Title: Biosynthesis, total synthesis, structural modifications, bioactivity, and mechanism of action of the quinone-methide triterpenoid celastrol

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses two acetyl-CoA molecules to form acetoacetyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports precursor formation for celastrol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome; transcriptomes; metabolic profiles (mentioned generally, no accession)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning and characterization from suspension cells
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for reconstructing upstream precursor supply in heterologous celastrol production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Triterpenoids including celastrol derive primarily from the cytosolic MVA pathway. 2. AACT catalyzes the first step from acetyl-CoA to acetoacetyl-CoA. 3. The T. wilfordii AACT gene was cloned and characterized.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a summarized core biosynthetic gene in this review; direct impact on celastrol titre not individually quantified.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: 2-C-methyl-D-erythritol 4-phosphate (MEP) pathway; isoprenoid precursor metabolism related to celastrol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate and pyruvate to form 1-deoxy-D-xylulose-5-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: provides exchangeable isoprenoid precursors potentially contributing to celastrol biosynthesis through MVA/MEP crosstalk
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning from T. wilfordii; expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate to enhance precursor exchange and isoprenoid flux in engineered systems
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TwDXS was cloned from T. wilfordii. 2. It is one of the first two rate-limiting enzymes of the MEP pathway. 3. It is highly expressed in roots, suggesting association with celastrol-related terpenoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MEP contribution to celastrol is indirect and linked through pathway crosstalk rather than dedicated celastrol steps.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: MEP pathway; isoprenoid precursor metabolism related to celastrol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports exchangeable isoprenoid precursor generation potentially feeding celastrol biosynthesis through crosstalk
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning from T. wilfordii; expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for balancing plastidial isoprenoid precursor supply in heterologous engineering
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TwDXR was cloned from T. wilfordii. 2. It is a rate-limiting MEP-pathway enzyme. 3. It shows high root expression.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: controls balance of prenyl donors used to form FPP for triterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: engineering target to optimize IPP/DMAPP ratio in celastrol chassis strains
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IDI catalyzes IPP/DMAPP interconversion. 2. The review identifies this as a rate-limiting step associated with protonation/deprotonation. 3. It influences supply of prenyl building blocks for celastrol precursors.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No specific T. wilfordii clone discussed here.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses DMAPP with IPP units to form FPP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: produces the key precursor FPP for triterpenoid/celastrol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: review summary of known pathway chemistry
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: important flux-control target for celastrol precursor enhancement
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: may affect sterol and sesquiterpene pools
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FPS catalyzes formation of FPP from DMAPP and IPP. 2. FPP is the key precursor of triterpenoids. 3. This step is central to celastrol precursor supply.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No specific T. wilfordii FPS characterization detailed in this review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts two FPP molecules into squalene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: essential for channeling isoprenoid flux toward triterpenoid skeleton biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: key enzyme for boosting triterpene flux in heterologous celastrol production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SQS converts FPP to squalene. 2. The review states it plays an essential role in isoprenoid biosynthesis. 3. It is a necessary step toward celastrol scaffold formation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes squalene to 2,3-oxidosqualene with NADPH-cytochrome P450 reductase participation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enables formation of oxidosqualene for triterpene cyclization toward friedelin/celastrol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cloning of five genes and functional complementation in CRISPR/Cas9-engineered erg1 mutant yeast
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: validated enzymes for heterologous celastrol precursor pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Five T. wilfordii SQE genes (TwSEs1-5) were cloned. 2. Their functions were verified by complementation in yeast erg1 mutant. 3. They catalyze squalene epoxidation to 2,3-oxidosqualene, a required precursor for celastrol scaffold biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper summarizes these as cloned and verified enzymes.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene into pentacyclic triterpene skeletons including friedelin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: produces the friedelin scaffold precursor for celastrol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: initial committed step of triterpenoid biosynthesis; branch point between triterpenoids and sterols
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: precursor feeding experiments confirmed friedelin as precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: precursor feeding experiments showing friedelin is the precursor of celastrol
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: major engineering target to direct oxidosqualene flux into friedelin/celastrol rather than sterols
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Oxidosqualene cyclization is the initial committed step of triterpenoid biosynthesis. 2. OSC-catalyzed cyclization is a branch point separating triterpenoid and sterol formation. 3. Friedelin was confirmed as the celastrol precursor by feeding experiments.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific friedelin synthase gene symbol is not provided in this review text.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes three-step C-29 oxidation of friedelin via 29-hydroxyfriedelin and 3-oxo-friedelan-29-al to polpunonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: converts friedelin toward polpunonic acid, a key downstream celastrol precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization of P450 oxidation activity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable enzyme for pathway reconstruction and precursor production in yeast chassis for celastrol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: wild-type P450 catalytic efficiency in heterologous strains is generally low
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP712K4 catalyzes C-29 oxidation of friedelin. 2. The oxidation proceeds through three intermediates to polpunonic acid. 3. This discovery clarified a key downstream step toward celastrol.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Polpunonic acid production is a foundation for future full celastrol biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes C-29 oxidation of friedelin to polpunonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports production of the celastrol precursor polpunonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: transcriptome (implied by screening from roots, no accession)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene discovery from roots; heterologous pathway reconstruction in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for engineered polpunonic acid production as celastrol precursor
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: low catalytic efficiency in heterologous hosts may require enzyme engineering
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP712K1 was reported from T. wilfordii roots. 2. Its function is identical to CYP712K4 in friedelin C-29 oxidation. 3. It was used in reconstructed yeast pathway producing polpunonic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of a functionally redundant CYP712K set.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes C-29 oxidation of friedelin to polpunonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports production of the celastrol precursor polpunonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene discovery from roots; heterologous pathway reconstruction in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate enzyme for polpunonic acid-producing chassis construction
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: low activity in heterologous systems may require engineering
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP712K2 is one of three additional T. wilfordii root P450s. 2. Its function matches CYP712K4. 3. It contributes to engineered production of polpunonic acid in yeast.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes C-29 oxidation of friedelin to polpunonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports production of the celastrol precursor polpunonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene discovery from roots; heterologous pathway reconstruction in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate enzyme for production of celastrol precursor in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: low P450 catalytic efficiency in heterologous strains
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP712K3 was identified from T. wilfordii roots. 2. It shows the same friedelin oxidation function as CYP712K4. 3. It was part of reconstructed yeast pathway yielding polpunonic acid.
